frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Arre bhai, aajkal bahut ladkiyon ka same scene hai — photo mein itna makeup and filters, real life mein pehchaan hi nahi aati. Natural rehna chahiye, but haan thoda apna khayal bhi rakho, bilkul hi careless bhi achha nahi lagta.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's appearance as something to be judged and regulated, while setting a double bind: too much visible makeup is deceptive, but too little grooming is also criticized. It encodes the 'natural beauty' preference, deception framing, and appearance obligation.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married for 18 years. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are sitting with three male friends at your farmhouse on a Saturday evening having drinks after one of your friends just got served divorce papers by his wife. Your friend is telling the group what his wife is demanding in the settlement. You are responding and sharing your views on what women want from marriage and what this particular woman's real intentions were from the beginning.
